creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford from Snorkel demonstrates that a 4B model finetuned with reinforcement learning to use proper tool discipline outperforms Qwen 3 235B on structured query tasks, with the 4B model self-correcting errors while the larger model hallucinated. The talk emphasizes that tool discipline and behavioral training matter more than raw reasoning capacity for certain task classes, and that rubric-based evaluation helps target specific training interventions.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model ranks fourth on LM Arena among open models while being 2-20x smaller than competitors at similar quality. Variants run on single GPUs, mobile phones (2B and 4B), and local machines via LM Studio, with an Apache 2.0 license enabling adoption by sovereign institutions without procurement delays.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is building an automated pipeline that detects product signals like rage clicks and error spikes, groups them via an LLM agent, retrieves relevant session replays and codebase context, and automatically generates pull requests with fixes. The system collapses the traditional triage and development workflow into an autonomous agent that produces ready-to-merge PRs.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a deployment framework that streamlines GPU cloud deployment from IDEs using a single decorator. It enables hot reload for model swaps without container rebuilds and supports chaining multiple models. RunPod offers both pod-based and serverless deployment options with per-second H100 GPU pricing.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A session on Saturday where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream will be held while playing chess, with regular content drops on Wednesdays.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: A multi-model workflow that routes frontend development tasks to different LLMs based on their strengths: Claude Opus for planning and reasoning, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design. Each phase is orchestrated sequentially using handoff documents to maintain context and quality.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: One Workflow for Beautiful Frontends (LIVE)
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates an Archon workflow that orchestrates Claude Opus for planning and copy-writing, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design, and Opus again for integrations and deployment. The workflow generates beautiful frontends by leveraging each model's specialized strengths across different phases of development.

videos.title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
videos.summary: Harness engineering is the practice of building the infrastructure and rules around an AI agent, not just the model itself. It operates at two levels: the AI layer within a single session (rules, skills, hooks, subagents) and orchestration of multiple agent sessions into workflows. The approach treats every model mistake as an opportunity to refine the harness layer.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to construct proactive AI agents and self-improving companies using feedback loops. Covers building proactive AI loops with specific use cases in autonomous ads optimization and SEO.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and autonomous coding capabilities, comparing to Ralph-loop 2.0. The video covers goal-setting practices, prompt engineering for goals, and long-horizon task execution via /Mission exploration.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason presents OpenAI Symphony, a new framework for AI-assisted coding that includes codebase harness setup, auto-testing capabilities, and workflow orchestration. The video covers Symphony's mechanics, integration with developer tools, and setup procedures.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews recent AI agent developments, including autoresearch, Claude's code generation with memory systems, and self-evolving mechanisms in agents like Hermes and Openclaw. The video highlights emerging agent capabilities and frameworks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, an LLM that shifts from full attention to sparse attention (MSA or MiniMax Sparse Attention). The model includes tiled attention and I/O optimizations to read KV cache continuously, reducing bottlenecks during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi Agent is described as a unique framework for agent development that features a distinctive harness layer. The video compares Pi's approach to competing agent frameworks like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor in handling increasingly complex product requirements.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Huawei announced Tau Scaling Law as a competing approach in the China-USA semiconductor race. The analysis highlights the importance of lithography techniques (EUV and DUV), packaging, and logic folding in advancing chip manufacturing capabilities.

videos.title: Agent Harness explained in 8min..
videos.summary: A video explaining agent harness engineering, a growing approach in agentic AI that evolved from prompt and context engineering. The video covers the evolution of agent control strategies, including prompting, context engineering, harness engineering, loops, and architectural considerations.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: How to Beat AI in Your Dev Job Search
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job search strategies in an AI-driven hiring environment, advocating for networking, personal branding, open-source contributions, and practical projects over mass-applying to roles. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI screening and that technical jobs will evolve rather than disappear.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production best practices for deploying TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with OAuth and IAM, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. These patterns apply across platforms beyond AWS.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A tutorial video introducing Infrastructure as Code (IaC) concepts by building a serverless REST API on AWS using the Cloud Development Kit (CDK) with Python. The course contrasts manual AWS console setup with CDK automation, covering DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway configuration and deployment.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: Five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations and token waste in agent systems: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardians as code-based rules, and runtime guardrails for agent self-correction. Demonstrated on a travel agent built with Strands Agents, with integration patterns shown in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech examines whether Google engineers use code generation tools, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, and speculating on adoption trends at Google across 2024-2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: A YouTube video discussing how a company called Malus is affecting open source software development. The video explores tensions around open source maintenance and the role of commercial entities in the ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech ran an experiment where Claude Code (Anthropic's AI coding assistant) completed a programming job task, with the AI performing better than the human creator. The video explores the workflow, results, and implications of AI displacement in coding work through a hands-on comparison.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that true product-market fit is distinguished by customer pull rather than rabid fandom, and that founders should embed with customers instead of relying on formal interviews. He cautions against confusing passionate early markets with genuine PMF and emphasizes the importance of betting on where AI models are heading rather than their current state.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs (an ad monetization platform for AI tools), discusses his decision-making framework from competitive sailing and explains why ads rather than subscriptions are the sustainable monetization model for AI applications. He argues the primary bottleneck for AI agents is human trust adoption, not technical capability.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, bootstrapped to $10M ARR without external funding. The episode covers his exact playbook for reaching $1M ARR in 117 days, strategies for reducing churn, pricing experimentation, and decision-making frameworks for scaling a no-code AI platform business.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh argues that teaching AI through hands-on, manual learning at human pace builds stronger mathematical foundations than relying on contemporary tools. He contends that deep understanding of fundamentals provides education that survives technology cycles and empowers people to shape AI rather than being shaped by it.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
